notes: Slice 1 cm thick. Steam until fork-tender; retains beta-carotene better than boiling.
Cube 2–3 cm. Roast plain — no sugar, no spice. Mash before serving.
Always cooked — raw sweet potato is hard to digest. Cube or bake whole and mash.
Florets only, no thick stems. Keep al dente — overcooking destroys vitamin C.
Wilt briefly in boiling water, drain, chop. Reduces oxalates slightly.
Trim ends. Steam until bright green and tender-crisp. Can be served raw too.
Slice or cube. Safe raw — cooking only softens for picky eaters.
Fresh or frozen (thaw first). Never use canned (added sodium).
Strip leaves from tough stems, chop fine. Sparingly — oxalates + thiocyanates.
Peel and cube 2 cm. Cook fully — raw parsnips are very fibrous.
Trim greens, scrub. Cook whole then peel; expect colored stool — harmless.
Halve, scoop seeds. Roast cut-side down on parchment. Mash flesh.
Serve raw — high water content. Slice into sticks; cooking ruins texture.
Chop finely into 5 mm pieces to prevent choking. Strings are hard to digest.
Trim woody ends. Steam until tender. Slice into bite-size pieces before serving.
Halve sprouts, steam until tender. Limit portion size — known to cause gas.
